terms.definition: The main public deployment-oriented model and app framing that emphasizes practical, artifact-backed retention screening rather than a survey-heavy or retraining-heavy workflow.
terms.preferred_usage: Use when describing the public model mode, the main app framing, or the preserved public reference selection.

terms.definition: An alternate builder mode that keeps stronger explanatory survey-style features such as burnout_index and effort_reward_gap, but is not the main public deployment framing in this repo.
terms.preferred_usage: Use when discussing alternate offline build modes or future comparison studies.

terms.definition: Runtime condition where the app reads checked-in generated V2 artifacts as the main source for metadata, row-level scores, model tables, or explainability outputs.
terms.preferred_usage: Use when describing the normal preferred runtime path in the current repo.

terms.definition: Runtime condition where missing V2 artifacts cause parts of the app, especially the Workforce Explorer, to use built-in deterministic heuristics and reference visuals instead of generated row-level outputs.
terms.preferred_usage: Use when describing resilience behavior or explaining why a page is not showing final model outputs.

terms.definition: The explicit builder rule that preserves the public operational source-of-truth selection of weighted XGBoost at threshold 0.29 even when a local rerun metric leaderboard differs.
terms.preferred_usage: Use when explaining why public canonical truth may differ from a fresh local rerun table.

terms.definition: The process of sweeping decision thresholds and reviewing precision, recall, F-scores, cost, and flagged counts to choose an operating point for retention screening.
terms.preferred_usage: Use when discussing the threshold curve, confusion matrix, and manager review queue sizing.

terms.definition: An early-warning review workflow for identifying possible attrition exposure that should support human investigation rather than automate employment decisions.
terms.preferred_usage: Use for the app’s business purpose and responsible-use framing.

terms.definition: A weighted exposure measure used to compare concentration of attrition concern, derived from attrition probability and salary cost weight in artifact-backed mode, or from heuristic screening score and cost weight in fallback mode.
terms.preferred_usage: Use when comparing department exposure or filtered employee burden.

terms.definition: A deterministic hashed row identity derived from cleaned row content, intended as the stable join key for row-level generated artifacts.
terms.preferred_usage: Use when joining artifact-backed row-level outputs to cleaned data.

terms.definition: Model interpretation output used here to explain which features contribute most strongly to the chosen model’s distinctions; it is interpretation support, not causal HR truth.
terms.preferred_usage: Use when discussing explainability tables, summary plots, or future drilldowns.

terms.definition: The future organizing method spine for this project: Plan, Analyze, Construct, Execute.
terms.preferred_usage: Use as the structural navigation and governance frame for future navigator, RAG, and orchestration layers.
